notes: Foundational Sportsmatic automatic, non-calendar (no day/date). Offered in 17/19/21-jewel grades; uses Seiko's bidirectional Magic Lever winding. 6601A from 1961, later 6601B revision.
Sportsmatic automatic, non-calendar (no day/date despite earlier claims). 6602B (c.1968) reached ~45h power reserve and was thinner. Derived from the Sportsman/957 line.
Early Sportsmatic automatic calendar movement (c.1962), part of the first-generation Suwa 5/calendar family. Documentation is sparse; date window present on calendar variants.
62-series automatic with date complication used across Sportsmatic models.
Day-date automatic in the 62xx family; mechanically related to the 6217 diver caliber.
Sportsmatic 5 automatic day-date (non-quickset), 21 jewels. 6606B launched 1965; traces back to the Suwa 410, Seiko's first '5' date movement. Direct sibling of the 6619.
Hand-wind 18000 vph 'second-generation' King Seiko caliber, Daini-built; the 5722-9990/9991 are the classic 'KSK' that inspired the modern KS reissue.
High-jewel (21) Sportsmatic 5 / Super 5 automatic day-date, non-quickset. One of the most common late-66xx calibers before the 61xx series took over; notably used in the sterile MACV-SOG watches.
Extremely common Seiko 5 day-date automatic of the late 1960s and 1970s; very long production run across many references.
Powers Seiko's first dive watch, the 62MAS (150m); part of the 62-series automatic family, sharing base architecture with the 6206 day-date.
Date-only Bell-Matic alarm caliber, less common than the day/date 4006. Window widened from the original draft's over-narrow 1968-1969 claim.
Day/date variant of the Bell-Matic automatic alarm watch; offered in 17, 21 or 27 jewels. Produced from 1966 until Bell-Matic production wound down in the early 1980s.
Day-date automatic workhorse with quick-set calendar, used across Seiko 5 and dressier 5 lines.
Higher-jewel day-date automatic used in Seiko 5 and Sportsmatic Deluxe pieces.
Hand-wind 18000 vph 44GS movement; the 4420-9000 case established Taro Tanaka's 'Grammar of Design' that defines modern Grand Seiko styling.

Day-date 51-Stream automatic (often dial-marked 'Presmatic'), 19800 bph, 33 functional jewels, hacking and instant date change; introduced 1967 to replace the 6218, with the 5146 as its hi-beat sibling.
Core day-date Seiko 5 workhorse of the 51-Stream family, 19800 bph, 23 jewels, bilingual day wheel and crown quickset date; one of the most produced vintage Seiko automatics.
Deluxe ('DX') 27-jewel day-date member of the 51-Stream Seiko 5 family, 19800 bph; introduced 1967 and phased out around 1969 in favour of the hi-beat 5146.
Early Seiko dive caliber; the 6105-8110 'Captain Willard' is the cushion-case diver famously associated with Apocalypse Now.
GMT/24-hour caliber; basis of the Navigator Timer and World Time models. The 6117B adds a hacking feature over the 6117A.
Grand Seiko 36000 vph hi-beat automatic with date, Suwa-built; the date-only sibling of the 6146 in the 61GS line.
Grand Seiko day-date 36000 vph hi-beat automatic, Suwa-built; the day-date version of the 6145 in the 61GS line.
Grand Seiko 36000 vph hi-beat caliber originally for the GS line; the 6159-7001 is Seiko's first 300m professional hi-beat diver.
High-jewel automatic in the 6215-7000 300m professional diver, predecessor to the 6159-7000.
First automatic Grand Seiko (62GS); date-only, with the signature recessed crown at 4 and no crown at 3 case design.
Day-date version of the 62GS automatic Grand Seiko caliber, sibling to the date-only 6245.
Hi-beat 36000 vph hand-wind base caliber for the 45KS line; no date complication.
Date-equipped variant of the 4500 hi-beat 36000 vph 45KS hand-wind movement.
Hi-beat 36000 vph hand-wind no-date 45GS movement, Daini-built; chronometer-grade adjustment.
Date version of the 45GS hi-beat 36000 vph hand-wind, Daini-built; one of the most collectible vintage GS hand-winds.
Date-only automatic in the early Lord Matic line, 21600 bph with quickset date.
Day-date automatic, 21600 bph, core Lord Matic (LM) workhorse caliber.
Day-date automatic 21600 bph used widely across LM and LM Special models.
25-jewel date-only automatic, 21600 bph, Lord Matic and LM Special.
25-jewel day-date, the flagship Lord Matic Special caliber, long-running across many references.
25-jewel date automatic used in Seiko 5 and 5 DX, 21600 bph.
25-jewel day-date automatic, Seiko 5 DX, 21600 bph with quickset day-date.
Hi-beat Grand Seiko V.F.A. (Very Fine Adjusted) date caliber, factory-regulated to roughly +/-2 sec/day.
Day-date Grand Seiko V.F.A. hi-beat caliber; the day-date sibling of the 6185 high-accuracy movement.
Entry Seiko 5 automatic with day-date, non-hacking non-handwinding; common Japan-domestic/Asian workhorse of the late 1960s and 1970s.
Single-date sibling of the day-date 7005 in the early Seiko 5 line.
Very Fine Adjusted (VFA) hi-beat 36000 vph hand-wind 45GS, Daini-built, factory-regulated to roughly +/-2 sec/day; top of the vintage GS hand-wind range.
Hi-beat 28800 bph 'Presmatic' day-date automatic, 27 or 30 jewels; the most advanced and last caliber of the 5100 family, introduced 1969 as the 5139 was retired.
Date-only Lord Matic (LM) hi-beat 28800 bph automatic, 23 or 25 jewels, Daini-built dress movement with instant midnight date change; evolved into the 5216 around 1972.
Higher-grade 25-jewel day-date variant used in Lord Matic Special.
Automatic chronograph with 12-hour register and vertical clutch; powers the Bullhead and John Player Special.
Among the first automatic chronographs; the 6139-6002 'Pogue' flew on Skylab. Single register plus day-date.
Seiko 5 automatic chronograph with day-date; the 7016-5001 'Monaco' is a sought-after reference.
Single-register Speedtimer automatic chronograph; the 7018 is the date-only sibling.
Grand Seiko 36000 vph hi-beat automatic with date, part of the 61GS Special era; used on a single reference.
Grand Seiko hi-beat automatic with day-date; the day-date sibling of the 6155 used on the 61GS Special models.
21-jewel Seiko 5 day-date variant of the early 70xx family.
Day-date Lord Matic / LM Special hi-beat 28800 bph automatic, 23 or 25 jewels, Daini-built; the most common LM caliber, successor to the 5206 with a simplified (non-instant) calendar.
Hi-beat 28800 bph King Seiko automatic of the Vanac era, 25 jewels, Daini-built; date sibling within the 52xx KS Vanac range.
Hi-beat 28800 bph day-date King Seiko / KS Vanac chronometer-grade automatic, 25 jewels, Daini-built, with crown quickset day/date and bilingual day wheel.
Hi-beat 28800 bph day-date King Seiko / KS Vanac chronometer-grade automatic, 25 jewels, Daini-built; top of the 52xx KS range.
Ladies-size 42-series automatic day/date; 17 jewels (lacks the additional bracket jewels of the 4207).
Ladies-size 42-series automatic day/date, 21 jewels, 21600 bph, roughly 24h power reserve.
JDM-only 150m diver with bilingual kanji/English day-date and hacking + hand-winding; predecessor to the 6309 Turtle.
Powers the cushion-case 150m 'Turtle' diver (7040/7049/7290); non-hacking, non-handwinding; also used in many non-diver 6309-8xxx day-date models. Very long-lived workhorse caliber.
21-jewel bilingual day-date Seiko 5 movement in the 63xx series; non-diver dress/sports models, sibling to the 6306/6309 architecture (the 6309 base is 17 jewels).
Successor to 7005/7006; ubiquitous Seiko 5 day-date that ran from the mid-1970s until superseded by the 7S26.
23-jewel date-only (no day) sibling of the 6306/6309, sharing the oscillating weight and dial-holding ring; used in some JDM divers and date-only sports models.
High-grade sports quartz powering the 7C43-7010 1000m 'Quartz Tuna' professional diver; the Quartz Tuna lineage kept this caliber in long production.
Basic 3-hand quartz movement used across countless Seiko 5 quartz and dress models; very weak for decade narrowing.
Robust sports quartz used in 200m/300m Seiko divers, sibling to the 7C43 Tuna caliber.
Ubiquitous 3-hand-plus-date quartz used in entry Seiko 5 and dress watches; extremely common, weak narrowing.
Day-date 3-hand quartz, one of Seiko's most widely fitted economy calibers; still in production, near-zero dating value.
Early AGS (Automatic Generating System) auto-quartz, three-hand with date; used in SUS/dress models before the Kinetic rebrand of 1993. AGS launched 1988, so window widened back to 1988.
Workhorse first-generation AGS/Kinetic, three-hand date; very common across the 1990s Kinetic line. AGS launched 1988, so window widened back to 1988.
Day-date companion to the 5M42 in the early AGS/Kinetic generation. Covered alongside 3M22/5M42 in Seiko's shared instruction manual.
Day-date economy quartz closely related to 7N43, fitted across Seiko 5 quartz and budget lines.
AGS-era auto-quartz diver-style caliber; AGS-branded predecessor to later 5M dive Kinetics. Window widened for safe intersection.
Alarm chronograph quartz from the 7T series, fitted to late-1980s through 2000s Seiko sports chronographs.
AGS/Kinetic caliber used in titanium Landmaster, Flightmaster (GMT) and Scubamaster models (e.g. SBCW001, SBCW005). Real refs corrected from the draft's placeholder 5M45-5A00.
Three-hand time-only (no date) high-accuracy 9F quartz, +/-10s per year.
Most common 9F: three-hand with date, instant date change, +/-10s per year; backbone of SBGX line.
Three-hand with date 9F variant; +/-10s per year high-accuracy quartz.
Three-hand with date 9F quartz, +/-10s per year; one of the original 1993 9F calibers.

Slim/small AGS-Kinetic with date and a press-button power-reserve indicator; used in ladies' and dress models (e.g. Age of Discovery) in the mid-to-late 1990s. Covered by Seiko's official 'Cal. 3M22, 5M42, 5M43' manual. Window widened.
Date-only sibling of the day-date 7S26, found in some Seiko 5 and entry models.
Workhorse non-hacking, non-handwinding 21-jewel auto powering the legendary SKX diver and SNK dress line.
23-jewel date-only counterpart to 7S36 in the 7S family.
23-jewel day-date variant of 7S26, used in Sea Urchin and various Seiko 5 Sports.
Alarm chronograph quartz with 1/20s chrono and alarm; workhorse of many Seiko and Pulsar sports chronographs.
Higher-grade 7S-series auto used in select Seiko 5 and SUS/Spirit models, often with open-heart dials.
Alarm chronograph quartz, successor/sibling to 7T62; very common in mid-range sports chronographs.

Undecorated, unadjusted-to-GS-grade sibling of the Grand Seiko 9S55; 28,800 vph, ~50h reserve. The long-running workhorse of the Marinemaster 300 and also powers the 1000m Emperor Tuna (SBDX011/SBDX014).
Defining early-modern 9S5x automatic with date, 28800 vph, ~50h power reserve; workhorse of the 1998 9S revival before the 9S6x update.
9S5x-family automatic (three-hand, no-date), 28800 vph; sibling of the 9S55.
Compact second-gen Kinetic (3M series) with date; documented alongside 5M62/5M63 in shared Kinetic instruction booklets. Window widened for safe intersection.

Kinetic in the 5M62/5M63 family adding a power-reserve indicator (second hand sweeps to show reserve when crown pulled). Confirmed by Seiko's official 'Cal. 5M54, 5M62 & 5M63' manual; used in Arctura-style models. Window widened for safe intersection.
Second-gen Kinetic, three-hand with date; one of the most widely used Kinetic movements. Documented in Seiko's official 5M62/5M63 manual; still serviced/listed, so endYear left open.
Day-date version of the 5M62; ubiquitous across the 2000s-2010s Kinetic catalog. Shares Seiko's official 5M62/5M63 instruction manual. endYear left open.
Kinetic with power-reserve indicator (press-button check); grouped with 5M62/5M63/5M65 generation. Widely used from the 2000s onward, so endYear left open.
Foundational GS Spring Drive automatic with date, ~72h reserve; debuted 2004 and still widely used.
Credor manual-wind Spring Drive with power-reserve indicator, ~48h reserve.
GS Spring Drive automatic GMT (date), variant of the 9R6x base, ~72h reserve.
Workhorse 50h-reserve automatic with Spron 510 mainspring and hacking/handwinding; powered the beloved SARB033/035 and original Sumo. Superseded by the 6R35.
Hand-wound 9S, 28800 vph, 72h power reserve; powers classic time-only dress models like the SBGW231.
Core 72h automatic with date, 28800 vph; 2006 update using MEMS-fabricated escapement parts and Spron alloys.
72h automatic with day-date, 28800 vph; day-date sibling of the 9S65.
Three-hand solar movement used in Prospex, Dolce, Alba and other solar dive-style watches. Window widened to avoid false contradiction; shares MT920 capacitor with V14J variant.
GS Spring Drive chronograph GMT automatic, column-wheel, ~72h reserve.
Hand-wound Spring Drive in the Credor Eichi line, Micro Artist Studio porcelain-dial pieces.
Higher-accuracy chronograph GMT variant of the 9R86, +/-0.5 sec/day rating.
Solar caliber used in entry and mid-range Seiko Solar three-hand models; shares the MT920 capacitor (~10-month reserve) with V147.
Workhorse 3-hand solar diver movement powering many Prospex 200m solar divers. Documented in the shared V131/V145/V147/V157/V158 instruction manual.
Three-hand solar caliber (improved accuracy and power reserve over V145A) used in dress and casual solar models. Documented in the shared V157A/V158A technical guide.
Solar chronograph caliber with 1/5-second chrono and 60-minute counter, widely used in Prospex Solar dive chronographs. Start widened to cover platform introduction.
Solar caliber with multi-function display used in mainstream Seiko Solar watches; uses MT616 capacitor with ~2-month reserve. Listed in the V147/V157/V181/V182/V187 instruction set.
Multi-hand solar caliber, sibling to V181, used in Seiko Solar dress and field models; shares the MT616 capacitor and is documented alongside V181 in Seiko's solar instruction set.
Hi-Beat 36000 automatic, 36000 vph, ~55h reserve; launched 2009 reviving the high-beat line.

Modern Kinetic with GMT/date and power-reserve check; used in Prospex Kinetic GMT divers (e.g. SUN043 50th-anniversary 'Yeti', SUN045). Start widened to 2010 to cover the full GMT-diver production run.
Premium ~45h column-wheel-era movement with date and power-reserve indicator, used in upper Presage models.
Solar chronograph caliber, sibling to V172, with 1/5-second stopwatch, 60-minute counter, date and 24-hour hand; ~6-month reserve. Used in Prospex and Speedtimer-style solar chronographs.
Hacking and hand-winding date-only auto, the modern successor to 7S25, ~41h power reserve.
Hacking hand-winding day-date 24-jewel auto, mainstay of the modern Seiko 5 Sports and entry Prospex.
Premium movement with power reserve and small date, found in mid-to-upper Presage enamel/Urushi models.
72h automatic with date, 28800 vph; widely used in current Heritage and dress lines.
SII export-grade twin of 4R35, date-only hacking hand-winding auto widely used in microbrands and mods.
SII export twin of 4R36, day-date hacking hand-winding auto popular in mods and third-party builds.
Power-reserve plus date complication movement with ~45h reserve used across premium Presage dress pieces.
Date-only / open-heart 4R variant without day, used in Presage and some open-heart automatics.
Hi-beat 36,000 vph 8L movement for the professional 1000m saturation diver, an 8L-grade cousin of the GS 9S85 hi-beat. Introduced 2014 and still in production.
Hi-Beat 36000 GMT automatic, 36000 vph, dual-time with independent hour hand.
Open-heart / no-date export twin of the 4R38, used in skeleton-dial microbrand pieces.
Power-reserve-complication auto in the 4R family, used in select Presage models.
Power-reserve indicator auto in the 4R range, found in Presage power-reserve models.
Manual-wind Spring Drive with 8-day (192h) reserve via three barrels; flagship high-end caliber.
72h automatic with date, 28800 vph; used in select Elegance/dress references.
First 9F quartz GMT caliber with independently adjustable hour hand, +/-10s per year; debuted on SBGN001/SBGN003.
Manual-wind Spring Drive, ~84h reserve with torque-return system, Micro Artist Studio finished.
Time-and-no-date variant in the modern 70h-reserve 6R3x generation, used in clean-dial Presage/Prospex pieces.
70h-reserve successor to the 6R15 via a longer barrel; now the standard mid-tier Prospex/Presage automatic.
Higher-accuracy +/-0.5 sec/day rated upgrade of the 9R65 automatic, ~72h reserve.
Higher-accuracy GMT counterpart to the 9R15, replacing 9R66 in newer GMT references.
Manual-wind Spring Drive, single-barrel ~72h reserve, used in slim Elegance dress models.
Modern open-heart power-reserve movement with ~70h reserve for upper Presage models.
Power-reserve indicator movement with ~72h reserve used in the current upper Presage lineup.
Refined-generation three-hand date 9F quartz introduced 2020 (time-only sibling of GMT 9F86), +/-10s per year.
5-day reserve automatic Spring Drive GMT, +/-0.5 sec/day, modern Evolution 9 series caliber.
5-day (120h) reserve automatic Spring Drive, +/-0.5 sec/day, thinner dual-barrel architecture.
Hi-Beat 36000 with dual-impulse escapement and free-sprung balance, 80h reserve; debuted 2020 in SLGH002 and White Birch SLGH005.
Modern movement with power-reserve complication in the long-reserve 6R6x family.
GMT export movement in the NH family with independent 24-hour hand, twin of the 4R34.
Manual-wind 9SA-architecture caliber, Hi-Beat 36000 with dual-impulse escapement, used in slim hand-wound models.

Newest-generation 8L with ~72h power reserve and rated +10/-5 sec/day accuracy (SPRON alloy). First appeared in the SLA081 60th Anniversary LE (2025) and became the standard movement of the non-limited Marinemaster (HBF001), replacing the 8L35 in modern 1968-heritage divers.
